back_translation: Now this was the lot for the tribe of Manasseh, because he was the firstborn of Joseph — for Machir the firstborn of Manasseh, father of Gilead. Because he was a man of war, Gilead and Bashan belonged to him.

And the lot was for the rest of the sons of Manasseh according to their clans — for the sons of Abiezer and for the sons of Helek and for the sons of Asriel and for the sons of Shechem and for the sons of Hepher and for the sons of Shemida. These are the male sons of Manasseh son of Joseph according to their clans.

But for Zelophehad son of Hepher, son of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh — there were no sons to him, only daughters. And these are the names of his daughters: Mahlah and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

They drew near before Eleazar the priest, and before Joshua son of Nun, and before the leaders, saying, 'The LORD commanded Moses to give us an inheritance in the midst of our brothers.' So he gave them, according to the command of the LORD, an inheritance in the midst of the brothers of their father.
Thus ten portions fell to Manasseh, apart from the land of Gilead and Bashan, which was beyond the Jordan.
Because the daughters of Manasseh received an inheritance in the midst of his sons. And the land of Gilead belonged to the remaining sons of Manasseh.
And the border of Manasseh was from Asher, Michmethath which is facing Shechem. The border went to the right (south), to the inhabitants of En-tappuah.
To Manasseh belonged the land of Tappuah, but Tappuah itself, on the border of Manasseh, belonged to the sons of Ephraim.

The border went down to the Wadi of Kanah southward to the wadi. These cities were Ephraim's in the midst of the cities of Manasseh, and the border of Manasseh was on the north of the wadi, and its outgoings were to the sea.
To the south was Ephraim's, and to the north was Manasseh's, and the sea was his border. And in Asher they met from the north, and in Issachar from the east.

And to Manasseh in Issachar and in Asher belonged Beth-shean and its daughter-cities, Ibleam and its daughter-cities, the inhabitants of Dor and its daughter-cities, the inhabitants of Endor and its daughter-cities, the inhabitants of Taanach and its daughter-cities, and the inhabitants of Megiddo and its daughter-cities — three of Naphath.
But the sons of Manasseh were not able to dispossess these cities, and the Canaanite resolved to dwell in this land.
And it came to pass when the sons of Israel grew strong, they put the Canaanite to forced labor, but utterly drive them out they did not.
Then the sons of Joseph spoke to Joshua, saying, 'Why have you given me one lot and one portion as an inheritance, when I am a numerous people, since the LORD has blessed me thus far?'

And Joshua said to them, 'If you are a numerous people, go up to the forest and clear for yourself there in the land of the Perizzite and the Rephaim, because the hill country of Ephraim is too cramped for you.'

And the sons of Joseph said, 'The hill country will not be enough for us, and there is iron chariotry among all the Canaanite who dwells in the land of the valley — both for those in Beth-shean and its daughter-cities, and for those in the Valley of Jezreel.'
And Joshua said to the house of Joseph — to Ephraim and to Manasseh — saying, 'A numerous people are you, and great strength is yours. There shall not be one lot for you.

For the hill country shall be yours; though it is forest, you shall clear it, and its outgoings shall be yours, because you shall drive out the Canaanite — though iron chariotry is his, though strong is he.'
